description: ADR-039 illustrative -- Movate fleet USAGE pane: throughput, agent-shape mix, provider mix, latency percentiles, top agents by volume. Tagged 'illustrative -- pending ADR 039 sign-off; assumes a customer template variable resolves either via Lighthouse subscription scope (Phase 1) or via the customer OTel attribute (Phase 2)'. Uses only instrument names confirmed on origin/main: mdk.jobs.completed (kind, status, tenant), mdk.job.duration_ms (kind, status), and agent.execute span attributes (agent, provider).

panels.description: Prescriptive runbook for the usage pane.

options.content:
### Usage + latency -- runbook

**What this pane answers:** how much MDK is being USED across the fleet -- runs/day per customer, what agent shapes (job kinds) dominate, which LLM providers carry the traffic, where the latency distribution sits, and which agents are hot. **Source instruments:** `mdk.jobs.completed` + `mdk.job.duration_ms` (with their `kind` / `status` / `tenant` attributes per `docs/observability.md`), plus the `agent.execute` span (`agent`, `provider`, `model_override`, `agent_version`).

**Normal:** runs/day per customer stays inside the customer's historical band; provider mix matches the customer's configured `providers/`; p95 latency by `kind` sits inside the customer's SLO (per ADR 027 D-SLO).

**If red:**
* **p99 latency spike on one customer, one kind** -- check the per-customer health pane and the queue depth on `capacity.json`; a stuck worker shows as in-flight rising without throughput.
* **Provider mix shifts unexpectedly** -- check the routing/canary state on `health.json`; a quiet model fallback (e.g. provider outage triggering a route to a secondary) shows here first.
* **One agent dominates volume on a customer** -- not necessarily a problem; cross-reference cost (`cost.json`) and quality (`quality.json`) before flagging.

**Caveats** (from `docs/observability.md`): `mdk` does NOT emit a per-route HTTP latency metric -- latency lives on `mdk.job.duration_ms` keyed on `kind` (the job kind), not URL route. `agent.execute.provider` is a SPAN attribute, not a metric attribute -- the provider-mix panel reads `AppDependencies`, not `AppMetrics`.

**Cross-refs:** ADR 015 (Langfuse), ADR 020 (OTLP -> AM), ADR 031 (dashboards-as-code), `mdk.run.tokens` lives on the `cost.json` pane.

panels.description: Daily runs/day per customer over the window. Counts terminal jobs from mdk.jobs.completed (all statuses). The customer split assumes the variable resolves to Lighthouse subscription scope (Phase 1) or the customer OTel resource attribute (Phase 2).

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.jobs.completed'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize Runs = sum(ValueSum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), cust
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Runs/day per customer

panels.description: Runs by job 'kind' attribute on mdk.jobs.completed -- the 'agent shape' axis: chat / workflow / batch / etc. (the values that flow through to status=success terminal jobs).

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.jobs.completed'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), kind = tostring(Properties['kind'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize Runs = sum(ValueSum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h), kind

panels.title: Runs by agent shape (kind)

panels.description: Provider mix across the selected customers. Read from the `provider` attribute on agent.execute SPANS (AppDependencies in AM) -- per docs/observability.md the provider split is span-side, NOT a metric attribute (mdk.run.tokens / mdk.run.cost_usd are keyed only on tenant).

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppDependencies
| where Name == 'agent.execute'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), provider = tostring(Properties['provider'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| where isnotempty(provider)
| summarize Runs = count() by provider
| order by Runs desc

panels.title: Provider mix (from agent.execute.provider)

panels.description: p50 / p95 / p99 of mdk.job.duration_ms over the window across the selected fleet scope. The histogram exposes per-bucket counts in AM as AppMetrics with Name = 'mdk.job.duration_ms' and a `le` label; KQL percentile() runs over the underlying timer values that Azure Monitor's OTel ingest preserves.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.job.duration_ms'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize p50 = percentile(ValueSum / ValueCount, 50),
            p95 = percentile(ValueSum / ValueCount, 95),
            p99 = percentile(ValueSum / ValueCount, 99)
  by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Latency p50 / p95 / p99 (mdk.job.duration_ms, ms)

panels.description: Top agents by run volume across the selected fleet scope. Counts agent.execute spans; each span = one run start.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppDependencies
| where Name == 'agent.execute'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), agent = tostring(Properties['agent']), agent_version = tostring(Properties['agent_version']), provider = tostring(Properties['provider'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize Runs = count(), AvgDurationMs = avg(DurationMs) by cust, agent, agent_version, provider
| top 25 by Runs desc

panels.title: Top 25 agents by volume

list.definition: Phase 1: Lighthouse subscription scope. Phase 2: hashed customer OTel resource attribute (ADR 039 D4).
list.label: customer
list.name: customer
